purpose: A navigation container that organizes content into labeled tabs with optional icons. Tabs can show counters and badges. Used as a secondary navigation within a page.
whenToUse: Organize related content into separate views at the top of a content area
Show category-based content (Header / Overview / Artifacts / Documents)
Allow users to switch between content panels without page reload
Display item counts in tab badges (e.g. "Artifacts (2)")
whenNotToUse: Inside a dialog — use a Form or Panel instead
Inside a table cell or list item
As the only navigation on a page — use SideNavigation for primary nav
When tabs exceed ~7 — split into separate pages or use hierarchical navigation
doRules: Place at top of content area below page header
Use Bold weight for active tab and Semibold Duplex for inactive — same size
Indicate active tab with 3px bottom bar in sapShell_Navigation_SelectedColor
Keep tab labels short (1–3 words) and consistent in length
Show count in parens for tab badges
dontRules: Do not nest IconTabBar inside another IconTabBar
Do not use more than 7 tabs — split into multiple pages
Do not change tab text via direct text node injection — use selectedKey + setProperties on IconTabFilter children
Do not communicate active state by color alone — use the bottom bar

layoutGuidance.placement: Top of content area, below DynamicPageHeader, full width.
layoutGuidance.sizing: Height 44px. Full container width with horizontal scroll if tabs overflow.

contentRules: Use noun labels for content categories (Header, Overview, Artifacts)
Keep tab order stable across visits

responsiveBehavior.M: Tabs visible inline; horizontal scroll if needed
responsiveBehavior.S: Overflow menu replaces inline tabs; selected tab + dropdown for others

accessibilityGuidance.requirement: Active tab text contrast ≥ 4.5:1 with background
Arrow keys navigate between tabs; Enter activates
Visible focus indicator on focused tab
Active tab distinguishable by more than color (uses 3px bar)
Each tab has accessible name from text property

exceptions: Inline mode (headerMode=Inline) places tabs in the page title area
For very wide tabs, allow horizontal scroll with shadows indicating overflow
